article_title: Encoded's ETX101 Cuts Dravet Seizures 76% at Dose Level 3 and Tracks Toward Neurotypical Development in Children Treated Before Age 2

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Exemplary clinical-data coverage. Every specific verified verbatim across all four snapshots: ASGCT 29th Annual Meeting Presidential Symposium on May 13, AAV9 + SCN1A target, intracerebroventricular (ICV) injection, April 10 2026 data cutoff, 76% MCSF reduction at DL3 with n=3, dose-dependent DL4 activity with sirolimus subgroup n=4, Vineland Adaptive Behavior Scales (VABS-3) n=9, Bayley Scales of Infant and Toddler Development (Bayley-4) n=11 at Week 16 and n=4 at Week 52, transaminase elevations as the most common AAV class effect, Sal Rico 'truly remarkable' quote verbatim, Mary Anne Meskis 'fear of every seizure and the heartbreak of watching development stall' quote verbatim, full FDA designation stack (Breakthrough, RMAT, Fast Track, Rare Pediatric Disease, Orphan) + EMA Orphan (BioSpace ASGCT release); 30-patient pivotal trial design, age 6 months to under 4 inclusion, sham delayed-treatment control, MCSF primary endpoint at 52 weeks, BLA submission framing (BioSpace FDA-alignment release); first-patient-dosed in ENDEAVOR Part 2, enrollment end-of-2026, initial data end-of-2027, Adam Numis UCSF Principal Investigator with 'promising open-label data from the initial Phase 1/2 studies generated significant interest' quote verbatim (BioSpace first-patient release); sirolimus tradeoff framing (STAT). VABS-3 / Bayley-4 use Unicode hyphens (‑) in the source but the article's ASCII hyphens are the standard form — no substantive difference.

source-0.status: Primary publication (Rule 9): Encoded's own ASGCT data-release press release via BioSpace. Verifies all efficacy numbers, safety profile, named quotes, FDA + EMA designation stack, full POLARIS trial structure.

source-1.status: Encoded FDA-alignment release. Verifies pivotal ENDEAVOR Part 2 design (30 patients, age 6-month-to-under-4, sham delayed-treatment control, 52-week MCSF primary endpoint), Initial Comprehensive Multidisciplinary RMAT meeting framing, BLA-submission target.

source-2.status: Encoded first-patient-dosed release. Verifies first-patient-dosed timing, end-of-2026 enrollment target, end-of-2027 initial-data expectation, Adam Numis (UCSF) as PI with 'promising open-label data' quote.

source-3.status: STAT News. Verifies sirolimus-tradeoff framing of the dataset.

editor_notes.content_quality: News-category (~937 words). Overview / What We Know / The Pivotal Trial / What We Don't Know structure. Clinical, precise, neutral tone. Strong 'What We Don't Know' section: explicitly flags small-cohort sizes (n=3 at DL3, n=4 at Week 52 Bayley-4, n=4 DL4 sirolimus-free subgroup), open-label limitation, conference-presentation-not-peer-reviewed status of the sirolimus mechanism. Appropriate caution on Bayley-4 trajectory durability beyond 52 weeks.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the Encoded POLARIS ASGCT readout and ENDEAVOR Part 2 launch.

content_preview.title: Encoded's ETX101 Cuts Dravet Seizures 76% at Dose Level 3 and Tracks Toward Neurotypical Development in Children Treated Before Age 2
content_preview.summary: An AAV9 gene-regulation therapy delivered durable, dose-dependent seizure control and early signs of neurodevelopmental rescue through 52 weeks, just as the pivotal study begins dosing.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Encoded Therapeutics presented an expanded dataset from its Phase 1/2 POLARIS program for ETX101, an investigational AAV9-based gene regulation therapy for SCN1A+ Dravet syndrome, during the Presidential Symposium of the 29th Annual Meeting of the American Society of Gene & Cell Therapy on May 13, [according to a release from BioSpace](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/encoded-therapeutics-presents-new-clinical-data-from-polaris-phase-1-2-trials-of-etx101-gene-therapy-in-drave...
